raw_text:
Slower, but glad that Spotify isn’t advertising for ICE anymore
Spotify playback is really lagging: songs, artist profiles, playlists, and podcasts are all consistently loading much slower than they used to. For how much of a drag this is, I think premium subscriptions are overpriced. I’m very glad that Spotify ceased advertising ICE recruitment ads, because I boycotted Spotify as a result of that (and encouraged others to do the same!) Once Spotify stopped airing advertisements for ICE recruitment, I did return to the platform because I enjoy accessing my playlists and podcasts from the same app. That said, now that I’ve returned, user experience has diminished due to the slowness and decrease in playback quality. Also, I wish artists were paid fairly for their work!

Excellent music
Ads are a bit long, 6 skips can be annoying, but this is a great music app! I can play all the music I want, but despite the ads, this is the music app I use the most! 5 stars, would suggest!

My main app for music; still deciding if I like the audiobook feature
My book I was reading was removed while I waited for my hours to be replenished. I just checked and it has been added back. The only issue is, now I do not know where I stopped because the book has been reset.

Reached My Limit
I’ve been using Spotify since 2020 and used to love it. I used to be very adamant about never changing music apps, but I’m at my limit. 30 seconds used to be LONG for ad breaks, but now they’ve been reaching two minutes. I also used to be able to listen to 5-7 songs straight without ads, but now it’s more 3-4–5 if I’m REALLY lucky. 

Music is one of my biggest coping mechanisms, however I cant even have 15 minutes to myself without an ad. In fact, recently, they’ve changed it so ads will play right after the first song when you decide to start playing music. Not to mention the additional non-audio ads that pop up around the app as well. 

Now, the contents of these ads is another topic entirely. I’m not a purist by any means, but I really don’t enjoy hearing ads sampling a suggestive piece of a smutty audiobook, ESPECIALLY when I’m with family. 

If I hate the ads so much, why don’t I just buy premium? I used to, but then the price rose. And again, and again, and again. It is now THIRTEEN U.S. DOLLARS (fine, $12.99) for a single person. That is ridiculous. 

Additionally, the yearly music wraps have significantly decreased in quality. They used to be fun and accurate, but now parts are very obviously incorrect, nor do they seem to capture activity from the entire year. This is a big disappointment because, as someone who prides themself in listening to a wide range of genres, I loved seeing which ones I had explored each year. However, I began to see less appear in my “wraps” despite knowing very well I had listened to more. Whether it’s due to Spotify shrinking/simplifying genre groups or a lack of effort, I may never know, but it’s frustrating regardless.

Originally I had contemplated one star, but the fact that Spotify does offer a free listening experience grants it another. Nowadays it feels like you have to give an arm and a leg just for the bare minimum, but thankfully this wide range of music isn’t behind a paywall… just yet.

Happy except
Overall, I am extremely happy with the Spotify app! I feel the algorithms for pulling in new music like what I’ve been listening to are better than the other music apps I have tried. The price is fair. The only complaint that I have personally has to do with playlist control. It is pretty hard to trim down a playlist for music/songs I do not want anymore, you have to do it one at a time it seems unless I don’t understand the controls. There doesn’t seem to be a general edit function for playlists. The other is for podcasting playlists. I would personally like to see smart playlist similar to those found the Overcast podcasting app. Being able to pull in specific episodes on a timeframe/delete after playing if they were downloaded/add multiple podcasts to a single playlist and having it do the same thing for those podcasts over time. Otherwise excellent app!

Emotional support 101
I haven’t written one of these reviews in so long. When I last wrote a review it was about how excited I was about a skip ad feature, I got premium and it changes a whole perspective in Spotify for sure, for free it’s also “free” and adaptable. The music quality is so good I almost cry to every song. There is so much more than music like podcasts and even those have the best quality. This app has been there through my highs and lows too, when I fell in love, and when I felt alone. No matter what Spotify was the music app that always came to my heart and I always felt a bond, not one where I could speak face to face but something that would listen to me just by me listening to the songs set out. Songs set out and recommended are very personalized, sure some are those “random song I dislike” however after long enough it turns into more “songs I really love” there is so many specific playlists made specifically for the user, I love that there is so many of them. I suggest someone use Spotify at least ONCE in their life, it’s really worth it. Thank you Spotify for being in my life

Advertisement Bombardment
Listening to music on this app is simply the highlight of my day, listening to the same Metallica songs constantly gives me an indescribable and surreal feeling of immense bliss. BUT, like all good things, it had to come to an end. For context, there is a 1:30-2:00 minute advertisement break per 10 minutes of listening. Anyways, the basis of my review is that the seemingly constant stream of stupid advertisements from the same four companies becomes redundant and ruins the overall listening experience. All though Spotify’s music and podcast catalog is top-tier, the advertisement bombardment is the one shortcoming that doesn’t put my review at five stars. Thank you for reading my rant about advertising. :3

Overall View of Spotify
Don’t get me wrong, I did enjoy trying other music apps, but Spotify is probably my favorite. I love how easy it is to find my favorite indie bands, musical songs, and songs from shows I adore. Honestly, I’m also a sucker for how easy it is to customize my playlists because I like knowing which playlist has what music on it based off of the vibe of its album cover. On the contrary, unfortunately there things I dislike, one of them being that I cannot download my playlists unless I pay for premium, which is something I REFUSE to do because it is honestly stupid that I should have to pay for something as simple as that. I also don’t like that I can’t take my music off of shuffle unless I have premium. It’s weird to listen to musical music out of order. The adds are understandable because Spotify needs to make money somehow, but I could definitely that becoming/staying as a common complaint.

Best thing on my phone
Spotify is my favorite app and probably the thing I use the most every day. I listen to music when I’m at school, at home, working out, or just relaxing. It has almost every song I want to hear, and it’s really easy to make playlists and discover new music. The recommendations are always good, and I like how it learns my music taste over time. Music helps me focus, feel better when I’m stressed, and have fun, and Spotify makes that possible. I honestly use Spotify more than any other app, and I can’t imagine going a day without it. I definitely recommend it to anyone who loves music.
